# WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0014 - QUIC RFC 9000 Connection-ID Retransmission Reliability Work Item

## Summary

Carve direct positive and negative proof links for send-runtime retransmission of packets carrying NEW_CONNECTION_ID and RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID payloads without claiming broader connection-ID lifecycle behavior.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0029
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0030

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0014

## Planned Changes

- Add focused architecture, work-item, and verification artifacts for the connection-ID retransmission proof.
- Link RFC 9000 Section 13.3 connection-ID repair requirements to the focused artifact set while preserving the broad RFC9000-0001 trace.
- Preserve the existing requirement-home tests that show loss queues repair and unrelated ACKs do not clear it.
- Preserve focused verification evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-cid-retransmission-reliability/`.

## Out of Scope

- Connection-ID issuance policy.
- Peer connection-ID registry mutation.
- active_connection_id_limit enforcement.
- Retire Prior To orchestration.
- Migration routing and path promotion.
- Stateless-reset token routing.
- Packet builder scheduling policy.
- Interop-runner migration success classification.

## Verification Plan

Run the focused RFC 9000 connection-ID retransmission requirement-home tests, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, verify reciprocal trace links for the focused artifact set, run `git diff --check`, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-cid-retransmission-reliability/`.

## Completion Notes

Completed as a proof-link closure over existing send-runtime retransmission behavior; no production behavior changed.
